records.text: South Africa's trip to Kanpur for the third test against India has given former England test cricketer Bob Woolmer the chance of a sentimental return to his birthplace.
His first cricketing sojurn to India was as a member of Tony Greig's England side in 1976-77.
His father Clarence Woolmer represented United Province, now renamed Uttar Pradesh, in India's Ranji Trophy national championship and captained the state during 1949.
Now aged 86, Woolmer senior lives with his son in Cape Town.
Woolmer's memories of Kanpur are few and blurred.
